item.title: Clicks shows off its BlackBerry-inspired phone in a new hands-on video
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: A new video shows the final production version of the upcoming Clicks Communicator, a BlackBerry-like smartphone that runs modern apps.

item.title: Nvidia competitor Etched hits $5B valuation, $1B in sales for AI chip
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Nvidia AI chip competitor Etched says it has already booked $1 billion under contract for the inference systems powered by its chip.

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 5 brings stronger agentic capabilities, lower pricing, and improved safety, positioning the model as a cheaper alternative to Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro.

item.title: Acti puts AI agents directly into your smartphone keyboard
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Acti is betting the smartphone keyboard is the next home for AI assistants. The startup's new keyboard for iOS and Android works across apps and lets users create custom AI-powered shortcuts using natural language.

item.title: Threads adds new features to Live Chats as it expands access
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The updates include translations, new tools for hosts, and more.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Science bets on workflow, not a new model, to win over scientists
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Science is a workbench that gives scientists one environment to do computational research, saving them from the need to bounce between databases, pipelines, and tools.

item.title: Blue Origin still doesn’t know why its New Glenn rocket blew up last month
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: But the company is still claiming that New Glenn will return to flight this year.

item.title: Tesla starts testing Cybercab without pedals or a steering wheel in Austin
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The company may finally be ready to try to deliver on Elon Musk's years-long promise of launching a robotaxi network of its own.

item.title: X now offers an MCP server to make its platform easier for AI tools to use
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X has launched a hosted MCP server, making it easier for developers to connect AI applications with the company’s API.

item.title: Arcturus could halve the grid’s electrical losses using its nano-infused metals
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Stealthy startup Arcturus uses lasers to infuse carbon nanomaterials into copper, dramatically improving its ability to conduct electricity.

item.title: Podcasting platform Riverside enters the newsletter publishing game
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Users will be able use AI to create newsletters based on their recordings.

item.title: Amazon launches new $1 billion FDE org, following OpenAI and Anthropic
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Engineers on the new team will embed within companies to deploy purpose-built agents, focusing on fast deployments and customer self-sufficiency.

item.title: Your brand deserves its own stage — Side Events at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: From October 10-16, host a Side Event and command the room during the week of TechCrunch Disrupt 2026.

item.title: Lumo, Proton’s privacy-focused AI chatbot, gets an upgrade
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Proton's Lumo 2.0 is dropping this week, giving users a broader variety of capabilities.

item.title: Crypto exchange OKX wants AI agents to hire and pay each other
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: OKX is bringing together payments, identity, and reputation into a marketplace for AI agents.

item.title: The AI jobs debate just got messier
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: A new report finds "high-intensity AI adopters” saw headcount increase 10.2%. Among those companies, entry-level headcount rose by 12%, countering the rhetoric that AI kills junior jobs.

item.title: Vibe-coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Wix-owned vibe-coding platform Base44 has started rolling out its own AI model — with hopes that it will eventually outperform frontier models.

item.title: Chamath Palihapitiya raises $135M Series A for his AI coding startup, takes CEO role
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: VCs remain thirsty to fund AI coding startups. This one, founded by investor Chamath Palihapitiya, is no exception.

item.title: Gemini’s personalized AI image generation is now free for US users
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Google is expanding Gemini’s personalized AI image generation to eligible free users in the U.S., allowing the chatbot to create images based on your interests and data from connected Google apps.

item.title: Watch out, Amazon: The Kobo eReader now has a Goodreads rival
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Kobo users can now automatically sync their reading progress to StoryGraph, making it easier to track books, reading stats, and challenges without relying on Amazon’s Goodreads.
